name: Perseids Meteor Shower
summary: One of the most prolific meteor showers of the year with up to 100 meteors per hour at peak. Radiant in Perseus — best viewed after midnight.

visibility.reason: New moon on Aug 12 — dark skies guaranteed.

name: Saturn at Opposition
summary: Saturn reaches opposition on Aug 27 — closest to Earth, fully illuminated by the Sun, visible all night. Rings are near maximum tilt for Northern Hemisphere observers.

visibility.reason: Opposition month — Saturn rises at dusk and sets at dawn.

name: Leonids Meteor Shower
summary: Fast-moving meteors from Comet Tempel-Tuttle. Up to 15 meteors per hour at peak. Known for occasional meteor storms in storm years.

visibility.reason: Waxing crescent moon sets early — good second half of night.

name: Geminids Meteor Shower
summary: The most reliable annual shower, up to 120 meteors per hour. Unusual source: asteroid 3200 Phaethon, not a comet.

name: Total Solar Eclipse — Aug 2026
summary: Total solar eclipse visible across northern Africa, southern Europe, and northern South America. Maximum totality lasts ~2 min 18 sec.

visibility.reason: Not visible from South Asia — path crosses North Atlantic and Europe.

path.summary: Totality crosses the Arctic, Greenland, Iceland, Spain, and parts of the western Mediterranean.
path.visibilityFromObserver: Not visible from Patna; travel required for totality.
